Ad Text:
The Company.
Sharp & Carter are partnering exclusively with a well-known Australian retail and consumer business on an Interim Commercial Manager opportunity supporting the organisation through a key period of transition and planned leave coverage within the Commercial Finance team. This organisation operates a large-scale, multi-site national footprint and is recognised as a market leader within the retail sector, with a highly commercial and fast-paced operating environment.

The Role.
This is a highly visible, hands-on commercial leadership role focused on maintaining continuity, commercial rigour and executive decision support across a critical four-month period. Reporting into senior commercial leadership, this role will operate as a trusted commercial partner across the business, ensuring forecasting, reporting and planning rhythms continue seamlessly while providing high-quality commercial insights to executive stakeholders. 
Key Responsibilities
Lead and maintain budgeting, forecasting and monthly performance reporting processes.
Provide commercial insights and recommendations to support ELT-level decision making.
Partner closely with Retail and Property stakeholders across the business.
Support analysis, modelling and financial commentary where required.
Ensure financial narratives and executive reporting remain clear, accurate and commercially focused.
The Person.
Experienced Commercial Manager / Senior Commercial Manager with strong hands-on capability.
Background within retail, multi-site or other operationally complex environments.
Comfortable operating autonomously with minimal onboarding or handholding.
Strong executive stakeholder management and influencing capability.
Commercially astute with the ability to balance strategic thinking and detail.
Systems exposure across SAP highly regarded.
Why Apply?
High-profile role within a nationally recognised retail business.
Genuine executive exposure and commercial influence.
Strong autonomy and trust-based environment.
Opportunity to step into a critical leadership coverage role immediately.

Do you enjoy delivering high-quality financial insights, modelling, and analysis to support strategic and operational decision-making?

Do you thrive in partnering with operational and Sales & Marketing stakeholders to drive performance outcomes and influence business decisions?

Do you want to play a key role in budgeting and forecasting, for a major project like the Mardie Salt Operation?

The Company

BCI Minerals is a values-driven company developing sustainable minerals for the modern world.

We are rapidly progressing the Mardie Salt Operation and Potash Project (Mardie Operation), a tier 1 solar evaporation project that will supply high-purity industrial salt and sulphate of potash (SOP) for more than 60 years.

Mardie will be the largest salt operation in Australia and the third largest globally. With first salt on ship on track to be achieved late next year, BCI Minerals is just at the beginning of its journey to become a key player in a multitude of globally significant sustainable opportunities.

Our values are the foundation of our success. They reflect our commitment to fostering an inclusive, high-performing culture driven by our people?s collective efforts and unique strengths. They guide us in achieving our strategic goals and solidify our position as a leader in the industry.

The Role

The Senior Financial Analyst provides high-quality financial and commercial insight, modelling, and analysis to support decision-making across BCI Minerals. This role is instrumental in

managing budgeting and forecasting processes, developing robust financial models, and delivering accurate reporting and performance analysis to enable informed strategic and operational decisions.

Partnering closely with operational and Sales & Marketing stakeholders, the Senior Financial Analyst plays a key role providing proactive insights on revenue, pricing, margins, and market trends, helping to optimise commercial outcomes. The position ensures strong financial governance while also contributing to continuous improvement across financial systems, reporting, and planning processes to enhance efficiency, accuracy, and overall business performance.

About You:

You are a commercially minded finance professional who thrives in complex, fast-paced environments. With a strong analytical mindset and the ability to influence stakeholders, you bring both technical expertise and a proactive approach to delivering meaningful financial insights.

Tertiary qualification in Finance, Accounting, Commerce, or similar
CPA/CA qualified (or working towards)
5?8 years? experience in a Senior Financial Analyst or similar role, ideally within mining, resources, construction, or large-scale projects
Advanced financial modelling, forecasting, and scenario analysis capability
Strong experience in budgeting and performance reporting
Advanced Excel and MS Office skills, with exposure to ERP/reporting tools (e.g. Pronto, Power BI)
Experience working with complex datasets to generate insights and recommendations
Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills
High attention to detail with well-developed analytical and problem-solving abilities
A continuous improvement mindset, including leveraging AI tools to drive efficiencies

The successful candidate will be required to provide a national police clearance that is not older than 3 months.

You must have current unrestricted working rights for Australia to be eligible for consideration.

What We Offer You:

We offer a range of benefits to our employees, including:

Permanent Fulltime Position in our West Perth Office
Working from our easily accessible West Perth Office, walking distance to Hay St
Attractive salary packages including annual and project incentives for all roles
Novated leasing
Shared parking on site ? pending availability
An engaged and supportive team that are passionate about encouraging each other to succeed.
Wellness and Lifestyle benefits, how does it sound having a day off for your birthday, or time off to celebrate culturally important or religious days?
Do you like to give back to the community? We also offer Community Service leave!
Wellness reimbursements (including for Gym / Fitness activities)
Generous Paid Parental Leave for Primary and Secondary carers
Company funded Life Insurance (including Death & TPD) plus Income Protection.

Supporting a diverse workforce

At BCI we encourage inclusive behaviour, that contributes to an environment and culture of trust and psychological safety where each individual can thrive and a variety of perspectives is not only welcomed ? but needed as they are essential to our success. We recognise that diversity strengthens us, and that true diversity values each person's uniqueness. We understand that no two careers or people are the same at BCI and we offer a great range of employee benefits to accommodate your preferred lifestyle.

BCI Minerals embraces diversity and inclusion and encourages applications from people of all backgrounds.

If you would like to be part of our team, and feel you have the relevant and required skills, we encourage you to apply now! We reserve the right to close this vacancy and commence recruitment prior to the advertising closing date. 

No agency referrals will be accepted at this time.

By applying for this position, you agree to the collection, use and disclosure of your Personal Information in accordance with BCI?s Privacy Policy which can be found at www.bciminerals.com.au.

Your new role

Conducting economic, financial and data analysis to inform pricing policy advice
Preparing high-quality briefings, reports and presentations for senior executives

Engaging with external suppliers/providers, state and territory governments, and stakeholders
Assisting in the development of policy options through research and analysis


What you'll need to succeed

Experience in pricing regulation, funding policy, or economic analysis (public sector preferred)
Strong analytical capability, including interpreting complex data and providing policy insights
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with experience preparing executive-level advice
Strong organisational skills and the ability to manage competing priorities
Proven stakeholder engagement skills and collaborative working style
Understanding of government processes and policy development
Tertiary qualifications in economics, econometrics or public policy (desirable)


What you'll get in return

Opportunity to influence national pricing policy
Work in a high-performing and collaborative environment
Exposure to senior government stakeholders and strategic policy work
Competitive APS6 salary and conditions
Career development within a specialised policy and economics function


What you need to do now

Job Title: Head of Student Insights and Analytics
Ad Text:
Full time, continuing position within the Advanced Analytics, Planning and Enterprise Data team
Lead the delivery of targeted insights to shape strategy, improve outcomes and transform the end-to-end student experience.
Offering a competitive remuneration package including 17% superannuation

About the opportunity

The University is seeking a Head of Student Insights and Analytics to set strategic direction and embed an insight led culture across portfolios and faculties, aligned to modern insights and analytics practices. Leading a team of analytics specialists, you will drive operational excellence, uplifting platforms, pipelines, reporting and visualisation for scalable, self service access, while translating insights into action through clear adoption pathways, measurable outcomes and continuous improvement.

This role is ideal for an Insights and Analytics leader with extensive experience in proactively scanning sector trends and market dynamics. The Head of Student Insights and Analytics defines and leads the University's institution wide insights capability, defining how evidence, data and intelligence are used to shape strategy, improve outcomes and transform the end-to-end student experience.


Your key responsibilities will be to:


proactively monitor emerging trends in higher education, student behaviour, market dynamics and market positioning
ensure the University remains ahead of sector shifts and is equipped to respond to emerging risks and opportunities
work closely with and advise members of the University Executive as well as other senior leaders
understand the requirements of each business area, translate complex analytical concepts into clear, actionable business insights, and promote the strategic value of data‑driven decision‑making.
act as an insights thought leader for the University by monitoring emerging trends in market research, analytics, student experience intelligence and data technologies
lead activities that strengthen the organisation's capacity to deliver insights at scale.


About you


working experience in a senior leadership role within an Analytics and Planning function within a large and multi-faceted organisation (tertiary sector and institutional knowledge is advantageous)
successful leadership and management of a high-performing team of analytics specialists with the ability to uplift capability, and build a culture of innovation, service excellence and collaboration
proven experience in identifying opportunities for innovation, championing modern market research and analytical practices, and ensuring the institution is equipped to leverage data
demonstrated capability in leading the uplift of reporting and visualisation practices across the institution, ensuring insights are delivered through modern, intuitive, consistent and scalable reporting channels
able to operate with a high degree of tact and professionalism across all levels of diverse internal and external environments
outstanding presentation, communication and influencing skills to drive key messaging and actionable insights across varied and diverse audiences.

Sponsorship / work rights for Australia

You must have unrestricted work rights in Australia for the duration of this employment to be eligible to apply. Visa sponsorship is not available for this appointment.

Senior Data Scientist | NSW Government | Contract

Our client is a major NSW Government agency seeking a Senior Data Scientist to join a high-impact Technology and Communications function on an initial 12-month contract engagement.

This is a genuinely hands-on senior role for someone who thinks statistically first and reaches for tools second. You will work across complex datasets to design experiments, build and deploy classical ML models and apply optimisation techniques that drive real operational outcomes.


What you'll be doing:

Designing and developing classical ML models, statistical analyses and optimisation solutions
Applying operations research techniques to complex resource allocation problems
Working across large, complex and sensitive government datasets
Conducting experimental design and rigorous statistical analysis to derive meaningful insights
Communicating findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Contributing to data governance and best practice within a multidisciplinary team
Working independently when given a task while collaborating with the broader team as needed
What you'll need:

Strong statistical foundations, experimental design, hypothesis testing, inferential statistics and the ability to infer meaning from data rather than just apply models
Proven classical ML experience across ensemble methods, time series, Bayesian methods or optimisation modelling
Demonstrated ability to lead your own research or projects from brief through to delivery
Strong experience with Python.
Experience working with large, complex datasets in a regulated or enterprise environment
Australian Citizen, Permanent Resident or NZ Citizen with a current NZ Passport (mandatory)

Highly regarded:

Optimisation modelling experience -- operations research, combinatorial optimisation or resource allocation
Government, law enforcement or highly regulated industry background
Academic or research background with published work
R proficiency alongside Python

Experience in IT Asset Management (hardware and/or software) within a large, complex organisation.

Job Title: Principal Engineer - Causal Inference and Experimentation Lead - Hawthorn East
Ad Text:
We’ve been proudly serving Australian communities since 1914 and have grown to become a top 30-listed company on the ASX with a portfolio of iconic homegrown brands. At the heart of our continued success is over 115,000 team members working across the country, all contributing to bringing our customers smiles. You’ll not only get to see your work make a difference, you’ll be part of a team working together to help Australians eat and live better every day.

 

About the team

 

As the way Australians shop continues to evolve, our Data and AI Team keep Coles at the forefront of modern retail. We combine data, artificial intelligence, and applied engineering to reimagine how our stores, supply chain, and digital channels operate — creating smarter, more connected, and seamless experiences for our customers and team members.

 

Operating as a centralised development and enablement function within Coles Technology, the team unites product, engineering, and platform capabilities to deliver the intelligence layer of the enterprise. We serve every part of Coles — from computer vision applications that enhance in-store experiences and reduce loss, to forecasting, optimisation, and decision-support solutions that drive efficiency, accuracy, and agility across our supply chain, retail operations, and corporate functions.

 

The scale and impact of this work are significant. Coles serves millions of customers each week across more than a thousand stores nationwide, and our AI and data capabilities provide the intelligence that underpins how we plan, move, sell, and serve. By combining deep data capability with applied AI and modern engineering, we’re enabling Coles to become a truly AI-enabled enterprise, transforming how we operate and how Australia shops every day.

 



About the role

 

We are currently recruiting for a Principal Engineer who will sit within the Data & AI Technology Hub and leads the design and build of enterprise measurement, experimentation, and causal inference solutions deployed across Coles’ business domains. Working in a hub-and-spoke model, the role partners with domain teams including Retail Media, Performance Marketing, Promo Effectiveness, and other commercial, digital, and operational functions to improve how Coles designs experiments, quantifies incremental impact, and makes investment decisions.

 

Key responsibilities will include:

 

Define the frameworks, standards, and roadmap to establish Coles measurement capability, and work with teams in Data & AI to apply the measurement capability and reduce fragmentation in how teams design, run, and interpret experiments.
Provide enterprise thought leadership on design, methodology, and decision quality.
Research, evaluate, and champion adoption of modern measurement methodologies including Bayesian inference, causal modelling (DAGs, difference-in-differences, synthetic controls), advanced A/B test design, uplift modelling, and multi-touch attribution frameworks. Success in this area is defined by reusable experimentation and measurement frameworks being adopted across multiple business domains, and a measurable reduction in fragmentation in how teams design, run, and interpret experiments.
Define and own measurement and experimentation standards and capabilities across Data & AI, including test design, power analysis, metric definition, and validation frameworks, driving improved rigor and consistency in how Coles designs experiments. Champion measurement excellence by setting the bar for analytical rigour, influencing architecture decisions for experimentation platforms, uplifting quantitative capability and participating in experiment and measurement design reviews.
Provide deep technical knowledge and hands-on leadership across engineering squads, guiding the design of experiments, attribution models, and business case measurement approaches. Act as the go-to expert brought into projects to advise on measurement methodology, audience/cohort definition, control group design, and causal inference strategy, enabling faster, more credible ROI assessment for interventions and campaigns
Influence and mentor data scientists, analysts, and engineers across multiple squads in measurement methodology, raising the organisation's quantitative literacy and analytical maturity
Build and maintain strong, trusted relationships with business partners, domain leadership, and cross-domain product teams. Be recognised as the person who makes complex measurement accessible and actionable for non-technical audiences.
Partner with marketing, commercial, and product leadership to embed rigorous measurement into campaign evaluation, use case design, and business case validation. Ensure consistent measurement methodology across Data & AI while delivering compliant, transparent, and trustworthy analytical outcomes.

 

 

About you and your skills

 

Experience working effectively in a dynamic, high-pressure environment
Experience operating as a senior measurement specialist in a fast-paced, complex environment, balancing deep-focus project work with, research and ad-hoc advisory across multiple teams
Experience collaborating with cross-functional teams (e.g. marketing, product, commercial, finance) to design and deliver measurement solutions that directly inform investment decisions and strategic priorities.
Proven ability to bring together diverse stakeholders (marketing, commercial, product, engineering) and align them on measurement approach, success criteria, and interpretation of results, including experience coaching analysts, data scientists, and business partners to think critically about measurement, ask the right questions, and interpret quantitative evidence correctly
Demonstrated ownership of end-to-end measurement delivery: from problem framing and experiment design through execution, analysis, and presentation of findings to senior leadership.
Track record of delivering production grade analytical systems, through individual framework development to full solution design and implementation
Proven experience designing and scaling advanced measurement systems: A/B and multivariate testing platforms, causal inference pipelines, attribution models, and business case validation frameworks that balance statistical rigour with practical delivery timelines
Expertise with modern data science and engineering toolsets including CI/CD (through DevOps/MLOps/DataOps), experimentation platforms, automated testing, and infrastructure-as-code.
Demonstrated experience of influencing solution design to enable robust and reliable insight generation, evaluation and experimentation.

 

Qualifications

 

Postgraduate qualification in a quantitative field such as Statistics, Econometrics, Bioinformatics or a related measurement science discipline. Post-doctoral or equivalent industry experience in applied measurement/causal inference highly regarded.
Ability to set measurement strategy in the context of Coles' broader direction, anticipating where the organisation needs measurement capability and proactively building the frameworks, tools, and knowledge to get there. Ability to balance statistical perfectionism with commercial pragmatism: knowing when a quick directional read is sufficient vs. when full causal rigour is required.
Expert-level knowledge of experimental design (A/B, multivariate, switchback, geo-experiments), causal inference (propensity scores, instrumental variables, regression discontinuity, difference-in-differences), Bayesian modelling, and Marketing Mixture Models (MMM).
Highly experienced in Python and/or R for statistical computing, with deep proficiency in causal inference libraries (eg DoWhy, CausalImpact, EconML), Bayesian frameworks (eg PyMC, Stan, NumPyro), and experiment analysis tooling
Familiarity with machine learning frameworks (PyTorch, scikit-learn), feature-flag systems (LaunchDarkly, Optimizely) and analytics platforms (Adobe, Google Analytics)
Strong architectural thinking for measurement systems: able to design scalable experimentation platforms, metric stores, and attribution pipelines
Ability to translate new research into practical business applications within the Coles context
Strong understanding of data privacy and protection principles as they apply to experimentation (e.g. PII in test/control groups, consent, data retention).
Outstanding communicator and storyteller: able to present measurement findings, methodology rationale, and strategic recommendations to audiences ranging from data scientists to the executive leadership team.

 

 

 



What’s in it for you?

 

Flexible working options: We know that work is only one part of your life, so we actively encourage a positive work-life balance and provide hybrid working options to help you achieve it.
Office perks: Take advantage of our gym facility and fitness classes, free parking, BBQ area, mini-Coles supermarket, fooderie hub where you can sample new products before they hit the shelves, school holiday program and so much more when you come in.

Job Description
Support the execution of the most complex, high priority strategic initiatives across the business with speed and precision
Play a key role in hitting our Group-wide value targets and shaping Endeavour’s future
Based in Sydney with hybrid working

As a Manager, Strategic Delivery you’ll be a core part of Endeavour’s new, high-calibre Transformation Office. We partner with senior leaders across the Group to drive our new strategy, and ensure we hit our value targets. 

Within the office, you’ll join a “strategic delivery” team that is deployed into business units for short, sharp stints to identify specific execution blockers, redesign delivery roadmaps, and instill the discipline needed to get key initiatives back on track. 

Here is a taste of what you can expect in this role:

Workstream Diagnostics: Lead the quantitative and qualitative "health checks" for specific workstreams, identifying technical, financial, or operational root causes for initiative drift.
Data-Driven Replanning: Build and stress-test revised project schedules and business cases, ensuring that recovery milestones are realistic and grounded in data.
Execution Support: Act as a "squad lead" during short-term intervention sprints, working directly with initiative teams to clear technical backlogs and unblock critical path items.
Performance Tracking: Own the "recovery dashboard" for assigned projects, providing real-time visibility into whether intervention efforts are successfully moving the needle on key metrics.
Insight Synthesis: Distill complex findings from diagnostic work into presentation-ready documents for the GM, Strategic Delivery to use in executive updates.
Process Improvement: Identify common "failure patterns" across projects to help refine the RDO’s diagnostic playbook and diagnostic tools.
Qualifications

Now let’s talk about you: 

Core Consulting Foundation: 4-6 years of experience in a top-tier management consulting firm or 5-8 years of experience in a dedicated internal Consulting/Strategy unit within a large corporate.
Track Record of Project Interventions: Proven experience being deployed into failing or stalled projects and successfully bringing them back on track to meet original or revised KPIs.
Complex Financial Modelling: Advanced experience building and stress-testing business cases to ensure financial logic and assumptions are robust.
Cross-Functional Navigation: Experience working within a "matrix" environment, managing stakeholders across Finance, Tech, and Operations to resolve cross-functional blockers.
Operational Change: Experience in the "last mile" of delivery - rolling up your sleeves and working directly with stakeholders to address and resolve identified issues.
Executive Presentation Development: Expertise in distilling key insights and producing crisp, high-impact updates for the GM Transformation and ELT-level sub-committees.

Job Title: Business Intelligence and Strategy Analyst (APAC) – Smart Access / IT Solutions
Ad Text:
The Opportunity:

This is a commercially embedded BI and strategy role working directly with the President (APAC).

The focus is simple: take fragmented data across sales, marketing, and CRM systems and turn it into clear commercial direction that influences how the APAC business grows.

While reporting infrastructure exists, insight is currently inconsistent across markets. Decisions are often made with partial visibility. This role exists to change that, by bringing structure, clarity, and commercial interpretation to the data that already exists.

You’ll sit close to leadership and be actively involved in discussions around performance, pipeline health, channel effectiveness, and regional growth priorities across APAC.

 

About the Business:

Our client is a global leader in electronic locking and smart access technology, delivering solutions used across commercial offices, education, hospitality, and multi-residential developments.

The organisation is well established internationally, with APAC identified as a key growth region. Australia plays a central role in that expansion strategy, alongside broader regional markets.

Growth is not the issue, focus is. The business has scale, data, and market presence, but is now working through how to better identify where to invest, where to accelerate, and where to adjust.

This role is part of that shift.

 

What You’ll Be Working On:

This is a hands-on commercial analytics role where your output directly shapes decisions.

You will:

Analyse sales performance across APAC markets, product lines, and channels.

Identify patterns in pipeline performance, conversion, and regional growth trends.

Partner closely with sales and marketing teams to assess performance effectiveness.

Work with CRM data (Salesforce) to improve visibility, consistency, and data integrity.

Build and refine dashboards that are actually used in commercial decision-making.

Support leadership with analysis for market prioritisation and expansion strategy.

Translate complex or fragmented data into clear commercial recommendations.

Surface risks, gaps, and underperformance early enough to act on.

 This is not a reporting role in the traditional sense.

 You will be expected to interpret, challenge, and guide, not just present.

 

What Success Looks Like:

Within 6–12 months, success in this role will look like:

Leadership teams making faster, more confident decisions using your insights.

Clear visibility of pipeline health across APAC markets.

Improved consistency and trust in CRM and commercial reporting data.

Identification of previously hidden growth opportunities.

More structured prioritisation of markets, segments, and channels.

 

About You:

This role suits someone who operates comfortably between data, commercial thinking, and senior stakeholders.

You will likely bring:

4–6+ years’ experience in BI, commercial analytics, strategy, or similar roles.

Strong experience working with CRM datasets (Salesforce highly regarded).

Advanced Excel capability and exposure to Power BI or Tableau.

Strong commercial instincts—not just analytical capability.

Confidence engaging with senior stakeholders and influencing decisions.

Ability to move between detail and strategic interpretation quickly.

Experience in multi-market or regional environments (APAC experience advantageous).

Backgrounds that tend to suit include:

Commercial / strategy analysts in corporate environments

BI analysts moving into more commercially focused roles

Management consulting professionals looking for an in-house operator role

FP&A or revenue analytics professionals with strong data capability

 

Why This Role:

You’ll work directly with the President (APAC) and be part of shaping how growth decisions are made across multiple markets. Your work will sit inside commercial conversations, not after them.

You’ll also gain exposure across a regional structure where decisions are still being refined, meaning there is genuine scope to shape how analytics and insight are used long-term.

If you’re looking for a role where your analysis drives action, not just reporting cycles, this is a strong next step.

 

Working Arrangement:

Hybrid working model (3 days office / 2 days remote).

APAC-facing role with regional exposure.

Collaborative but commercially driven leadership environment.

$120,000 – $150,000 + Super (based on experience).

 

Next Steps:

If you’re looking for a role where BI and commercial strategy intersect—and where your insights will directly influence regional decision-making, this is worth a conversation.

Apply now or send your CV (Word format) to dav••@salexo.com.au

For a confidential discussion, call (02) •••• •622 (zero Two Nine Nine Two Two Nine Six Two Two)

 

*Shortlisted candidates will be contacted within two business days.

Australia’s leading experiential tourism group, Journey Beyond, is privileged to operate 20 brands spanning the country and New Zealand, connecting guests to the land, and to each other.

Headquartered in Adelaide, Journey Beyond formed in 2016 and now comprises tourism brands including: iconic trains The Ghan, Indian Pacific, Great Southern, The Overland; premium small-group outback operator Outback Spirit; eco-luxury lodge Sal Salis Ningaloo Reef; aquatic adventures Cruise Whitsundays, Rottnest Express, Sailaway Port Douglas, Horizontal Falls Seaplane Adventures, Darwin Harbour Cruises and Journey Beyond Cruise Sydney; our tallest members, Melbourne Skydeck and Eureka 89; the historic Vintage Rail Journeys; The Telegraph Station in Alice Springs; Monarto Safari Resort; Exmouth Dive & Whalesharks Ningaloo, New Zealand coach tour specialist Grand Pacific Tours, and Voyages Tourism Australia.

At UNSW, we pride ourselves on being a workplace where the best people come to do their best work.  We aspire to be Australia’s global university, improving and transforming lives through excellence in research, outstanding education and a commitment to advancing a just society. 

Why your role matters

The Data Privacy Lead establishes UNSW’s enterprise approach to privacy, embedding "privacy by design" across the University’s data and technology ecosystem. This senior specialist designs the data privacy framework to ensure compliant use of sensitive information in research, teaching, and operations.

Collaborating with Legal, Cyber Security, and Data Governance, the role manages privacy obligations throughout the data lifecycle. The position provides expert advisory on legislative requirements and impact assessments to drive continuous improvement. Reporting to the Director of Data Privacy and Security, this is a dedicated specialist role with no direct reports.

About the Role

The successful applicant will be required to undertake and have experience in the following areas of expertise:

Establish and lead the enterprise data privacy framework by developing standards and controls for the responsible handling of sensitive data.
Integrate privacy-by-design into technology and data projects, partnering with Legal, Cyber Security, and Data Governance to ensure early intervention.
Serve as an authoritative advisor to senior stakeholders and researchers on compliant data collection, sharing, and de-identification practices.
Oversee and guide Privacy and Data Protection Impact Assessments (PIAs/DPIAs) to identify and mitigate risks for high-profile initiatives.
Maintain enterprise privacy policies and guidance materials, ensuring alignment with evolving legislation and regulatory expectations.
Monitor privacy compliance indicators and report insights to the Chief Data Officer and relevant governance forums to drive continuous improvement.

About You

Tertiary qualifications in data privacy, information governance, or a related field, complemented by relevant professional experience.
Expert knowledge of Australian and international privacy laws (Privacy Act, GDPR, PPIP, HRIP) and best-practice management frameworks.
Proven experience developing and implementing privacy frameworks, policies, and operational processes within a complex organisational environment.
Demonstrated ability to manage Privacy Impact Assessments and provide practical advice on compliance and risk mitigation.
Strong understanding of the intersection between data governance, cybersecurity, and information management regarding privacy assurance.
Exceptional stakeholder engagement skills with the ability to influence and partner effectively across technical, legal, and business domains.

For further information on the role & responsibilities, please refer to the Position Description

How to Apply: Submit your CV & cover letter detailing your interest and suitability for the job (as per the skills & experience bullet points in the Position Description) before Sunday 31st April by 11:55pm.


Please note: Sponsorship is not available for this role, valid Australian working rights are required on application.

 

UNSW Benefits and Culture: People are at the core of everything we do. We recognise it is the contributions of our staff who make UNSW one of the best universities in Australia and the world. Our benefits include: 

Flexible Working Options (work from home, flexible hours etc) 
Additional 3 days leave during December festive period.
Career development opportunities 
Up to 50% discount on UNSW courses
Flexible 17% Superannuation contributions, additional leave-loading payments and salary sacrifice.
Discounts and entitlements (retail, education, fitness passport)

UNSW is committed to equity diversity and inclusion. Applications from women, people of culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds, those living with disabilities, members of the LGBTIQ+ community; and people of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander descent, are encouraged. UNSW provides workplace adjustments for people with disability, and access to flexible work options for eligible staff.

Pre-Employment Checks

Aligned with UNSW’s focus on cultivating a workplace defined by safety, ethical conduct, and strong integrity preferred candidates will be required to participate in a combination of pre-employment checks relevant to the role they have applied for.

These pre-employment checks may include a combination of some of the following checks:

National and International Criminal history checks
Entitlement to work and ID checks
Working With Children Checks
Completion of a Gender-Based Violence Prevention Declaration
Verification of relevant qualifications
Verification of relevant professional membership
Employment history and reference checks
Financial responsibility assessments/checks.
Medical Checks and Assessments

Compliance with the necessary combination of these checks is a condition of employment at UNSW.

If you live in Australia or New Zealand, you've likely heard of AMP. But at a time when society is changing, we are too. We're now a nimbler business with new leadership and thinking.

For us, these are exciting times. There's a real potential for big thinkers to help us redefine what financial services could be. And turn our legacy into something even more positive and powerful for the future.

Help people create their tomorrow, while you create yours

We help people with their banking, super, retirement and finances. Through upturns, downturns, recessions, and major life transitions. Every day, we help people see and make more of their financial potential, so that they can create their tomorrow. And we've been doing it for over 170 years.

If we do our job well, we genuinely add to the prosperity of our country and its people.

How you'll make an impact

As a Senior Systems Analyst in Finance Systems, you will play a pivotal role in ensuring the stability, integrity and performance of AMP's core finance platforms. Sitting at the intersection of Finance and Technology, you will underpin the organisation's ability to deliver accurate financial processing, robust governance and reliable reporting outcomes, directly influencing audit performance, operational risk and business confidence in financial data.

You will act as the trusted subject matter expert across PeopleSoft Financials and the broader finance systems landscape, ensuring critical accounting processes are supported seamlessly through periods such as month-end, year-end and regulatory reporting. Beyond BAU, you will drive meaningful improvement, identifying opportunities to simplify, automate and modernise finance processes, while supporting key transformation initiatives. Through your expertise, you will help strengthen control frameworks, uplift system capability and enable Finance to operate with greater efficiency and confidence.

What you'll bring to the role

You bring deep, hands-on expertise in enterprise finance systems, with strong experience supporting PeopleSoft Financials in a complex environment. You understand how core accounting processes such as General Ledger, Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable and Asset Management, come together within the system and you are confident navigating both functional and technical layers to resolve issues and deliver enhancements.

Your analytical capability is a key strength, with experience leveraging data (including SQL) to diagnose issues, validate outputs and deliver sustainable solutions. You bring a strong understanding of financial controls, reporting requirements and governance expectations, alongside the ability to balance risk, audit requirements and operational efficiency.

You'll thrive here if…

If you can adapt from BAU to the ambiguous with ease, you'll do well here. Change is never easy, so bring your commitment, grit and growth mindset.

Because we run lean, you'll be expected to jump in and deliver across a variety of areas. Meaning, you'll be closer to the action and executive decisions that influence where we go next.

If you're someone that can hold their own, you'll find AMP quite liberating.

Why we think you'll love working at AMP

Doing what we've always done is not an option, so your clever ideas will get airtime here. You'll be encouraged to speak up and try new things. If they don't work, we move on - better for it.

We know there's no one way of doing things. So, you won't have to sacrifice who you are or how you work to fit in here. We're inclusive and flexible in many of the ways you'd expect. And in some of the ways you wouldn't. As long as your health and wellbeing come first - at home and at work.

In fact, most of what makes AMP such a welcoming, enjoyable place to work are our people. Wherever you go, you'll find moments to connect, feel valued and do meaningful work.

Whether it's through our first-class leaders who are invested in you and your success. Through year-round opportunities to volunteer, fundraise and give back to the community. Or in the everyday challenges you face as we work together to strengthen this great organisation. Challenges that will stretch you, amplify your potential and compound the impact you have.

We believe in the power of inclusion and diversity

We're dedicated to fostering inclusion, diversity, and a warm feeling of belonging at AMP. It sparks creativity, ignites innovation, and turns up the dial on the quality of our decisions and performance. This not only makes our workplace more engaged, but also leads to better connections with our customers.

We're your allies in the search for the perfect fit - when you apply, let us know how we can support you to put your best self forward during our selection process.

We're also committed to enhancing employment opportunities for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, so we enthusiastically encourage candidates from these backgrounds to apply and explore our Reconciliation Plan on our website

Ready to create your tomorrow?

If you're someone who sees opportunity where others see challenge, come and work with us in smart, progressive ways as we transform an iconic Australian brand for the future. And, through a series of career-defining moments, create your own tomorrow.

Don't procrastinate! We review applications when we get them, and if we discover the ideal candidate, we may close the role earlier than the advertised close date.

Looking forward to meeting you.

Experience developing, deploying and monitoring models in cloud-based platforms, with AWS experience preferred.

Ability to connect business needs with appropriate technical solutions and communicate trade-offs clearly.

Strong analytical and numerical reasoning skills.

Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 

Strong attention to detail, with the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously.

Ability to work in a dynamic environment, with the flexibility to reprioritise as business needs change.

Desirable Experience

Financial services, fintech, lending or credit risk experience.

Exposure to modern AI techniques, including practical LLM-related use cases.

The opportunity for internal promotion and growth within the business

On-the-job training and in-house professional development

A positive work environment and excellent office space, with free coffee and fruit!

Entry to our Employee Benefit Portal offering discounts, perks, and wellness resources

Job Title: Senior Data Program Manager - Data and Analytics
Ad Text:
Company description:



Come and join a thriving company and become part of a diverse global collective of free-thinkers, entrepreneurs and industry experts who are all driven to use technology to reimagine what's possible. Capgemini. Get the future you want.

For more about why Capgemini https://www.capgemini.com/au-en/careers/why-join-capgemini/



Job description:

About the Service Line:

Capgemini Global Insights & Data business line is a market leader in Data Engineering, Cloud Data Platforms, Data Science, and AI and Advanced Analytics across all sectors including financial services, public sector, consumer products, telecommunication & energy resources. Our offerings include end-2-end data integration to cloud platforms, complete suite of data engineering capabilities and a category of sector-based advanced analytics and AI driven solutions.

This is a unique opportunity within the Insights & Data (I&D) Global Business Line (GBL) to help grow the data, AI and Data Science engagements across all sectors in Australia and New Zealand. You will help shape and deliver complex projects and programs of work for our clients. You will understand our client's need to bring maximum value, impact, and innovation to the market as you oversee the delivery of client engagements for data and AI initiatives.



About the Role:

You will be considered a subject matter expert in Insights & Data Management, Architecture and solutioning. Your role will see you working across complex businesses, reengineering development projects to solve organizational problems through innovative IT solutions. You'll experience variety, one day you could be working with the client to understand their business requirements, the next day you may be working with your team on how to resolve a complex business or technical challenge to keep a project on track.

Key Responsibilities:

Working closely with key client stakeholders, you'll manage a team to deliver complex data projects on time and on budget with a commercial eye on future opportunities.
You will take responsibility for the people, the project delivery and aiming delivery excellence.
Clear obstacles and impediments blocking the team and protect the team from external interruptions and distractions;
Assist in engaging with the business customers on business processes and practices associated with the IT systems that implement, support or enable those processes and practices.
Define project objectives, deliverables, critical success factors, assumptions, global and regional considerations, and resource requirements.
Manage the delivery of projects across the full lifecycle, to required cost, timescale and quality parameters
Define project prioritization, cost/benefit, potential risks, and feasibility.
Ability to work with Business & IT stakeholders through open dialogues that manages their expectations & helps set agreed objectives
Should be able to motivate & influence team members for accountability ensuring excellent deliverables.
Required Skills & Experience
10+ years of Project Management experience delivering projects/programmes across one or more capabilities in the Data and Analytics space.
Understanding of the software development life cycle processes, experience of working in a large-scale program
Have the ability to influence and change if required existing delivery practices to allow efficient business value delivery
Strong stakeholder engagement experience in complex environments with a diverse set of stakeholders
Have an ability to facilitate alternative solution approaches using a consultative style and obtain agreed consensus across a broad stakeholder group
Market standard project management certifications are a must
You will have successfully delivered large data implementation projects
Managed Master Data Management/Analytics programs would be advantageous;
Agile/Scrum experience is a plus
Experience of Project planning, Issue, risk and change management
Experience of commercial negotiation for project delivery services
Experience of management of delivery to budget, and associated financial reporting / forecasting to ensure you are aware of the financial performance of your projects
You will have coached, developed and people managed teams of at least 15 to 20 people
Excellent communication skills with proven stakeholder management
Proven capability to contribute to sales activities for securing new projects and work - contributing to proposals, creating priced delivery propositions and presenting these to clients
Ideally, candidate would have CSI (Complex System Integration) project experiences as this project will cover: Data Engineering/ingestion, Data Quality, Data Governance and Master Data Management


What You'll Love About Working Here:
Shape the future of AI-driven transformation at scale in a collaborative, inclusive culture.
Lead, influence, and innovate while driving client outcomes and your own career growth.
Collaborate with a diverse community of AI, data, and technology leaders across Australia and globally.
Deliver responsible, sustainable AI solutions with real-world impact.

The Company

Our client is a leading professional services organisation with a strong national presence and global connectivity, known for partnering with some of the country's most complex and high-profile businesses.

Operating in a fast-paced, highly matrixed environment, the firm continues to evolve how it delivers value to clients, with a strong focus on technology, data, and AI-enabled ways of working. As part of this, there is a growing emphasis on how work is structured, how capability is deployed, and how the workforce adapts to ongoing change.

With a clear strategic agenda and strong leadership sponsorship, the organisation is investing in its workforce strategy and operating model, creating a unique opportunity to shape how work is designed and delivered into the future.

The Opportunity

This is a newly created, high-impact  function, working at the intersection of business strategy, workforce planning, organisational design, and transformation. The core focus of the role is to design and embed a scalable strategic workforce planning approach across the business, while leading initiatives that reshape how work is performed in response to AI, automation, and evolving client needs.

A key part of the role will involve breaking down existing workflows across different business areas, identifying opportunities for automation or simplification, and redesigning roles, structures, and capability models to align with future requirements. Alongside this, you will play a central role in leading organisation design initiatives, ensuring that structures, roles, and governance frameworks are aligned to strategic priorities and enable effective execution at scale.

This role will also partner closely with analytics, talent, and learning teams to translate workforce insights into practical action, including reskilling, redeployment, and capability uplift across the business.

Key areas of focus include:

Designing and embedding a firm-wide strategic workforce planning methodology
Leading organisation design initiatives aligned to evolving operating models
Assessing the impact of AI and automation on roles, workflows, and workforce composition
Facilitating workshops with business leaders to deconstruct work and redesign processes
Building practical frameworks, playbooks, and tools to support workforce and work design
Identifying capability gaps and partnering on reskilling and workforce transition strategies
Developing workforce insights, reporting, and scenario modelling to inform decision-making
Supporting the evolution of career architecture and internal mobility frameworks
About You

You bring a blend of strategic thinking and practical delivery, with experience operating in complex environments where workforce, operating model, and business strategy intersect. You are comfortable navigating ambiguity and building from the ground up, with the ability to take concepts and translate them into structured, actionable outcomes. You are equally confident facilitating workshops, influencing senior stakeholders, and working hands-on to deliver meaningful change.

You are naturally curious about how technology is reshaping work and are motivated by the opportunity to play a role in designing what the future workforce looks like.

You will ideally bring:

Experience in strategic workforce planning, workforce transformation, or workforce strategy
Exposure to organisational design, operating models, or role design
A background in consulting, transformation, or enterprise HR strategy roles
Strong facilitation and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to influence without authority
High data literacy, with experience interpreting insights and supporting data-led decisions
End-to-end transformation or program delivery experience
Experience in large, complex, matrixed organisations
Exposure to AI, automation, or technology-enabled workforce change is highly regarded
This role is well suited to someone from a consulting or enterprise environment looking to step into a role with broader ownership and direct impact on workforce strategy at scale.

The Process

The recruitment process will involve an initial meeting with Antony Jones, prior to client interviews, references, background checks and offer. During this process, you will have the opportunity to meet key stakeholders, gain insight into the team and environment, and explore how you can contribute to this critical transformation agenda.

For any confidential enquiries, please contact Antony Jones on 04•• ••• 544 or ant•••@levyl.com.au

Levyl encourage diverse, equitable and inclusive recruitment solutions and commit to respond to every applicant. We make selection and shortlist decisions based on your skills, experience, personal attributes, and motivators. Should you be successful for interview, we will tender you an interview accommodation checklist, so that you can let us know how we can support you to perform at your best.

About the Department

The Department of Agriculture, Fisheries and Forestry (DAFF) is an Australian Government department committed to building stronger, more sustainable agriculture, fisheries, forestry and land care sectors. DAFF works to enhance the value, productivity and profitability of these industries - creating and maintaining export opportunities, managing biosecurity risks, and supporting Australia's ambition of a $100 billion agricultural sector by 2030.

About the Role

The Business Analyst supports the delivery of the Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) by leading business process analysis and requirements definition across diagnostic laboratories. The role ensures business needs are clearly articulated, future‑state processes are defined, and solutions align with program outcomes, accreditation requirements, and enterprise architecture. The position works closely with laboratory subject matter experts (SMEs), the LIMS Product Owner, the Project Manager, ICT teams, and governance bodies to translate operational needs into clear, testable, and traceable business requirements.

Key Duties

Lead end‑to‑end business analysis activities for the LIMS project, including discovery, analysis, documentation, validation, and change impact assessment.

Elicit, analyse, and document business and functional requirements, ensuring alignment with laboratory operations, regulatory obligations, and program objectives.
Develop high‑quality analysis artefacts, such as:
Business requirements and functional specifications
Process maps and workflow diagrams
Use cases, user stories, and acceptance criteria
Gap analyses and options analysis papers
Work closely with ICT, vendors, and design authorities to ensure requirements are clearly understood, technically feasible, and traceable through design, build, and testing phases.
Support User Acceptance Testing (UAT) by contributing to test scenario development, traceability, and defect triage.
Identify and manage business analysis risks, issues, and dependencies, escalating as appropriate through project and program governance.
Contribute to change management activities, including stakeholder engagement, preparation of supporting materials, and transition to business‑as‑usual arrangements.
Maintain alignment with Laboratory Reform Program governance frameworks, reporting requirements, and decision‑making processes.

Required Skills, Knowledge and Experience

Essential

Demonstrated experience as a Business Analyst on complex ICT or digital transformation projects, preferably in regulated or operational environments.
Strong capability in business process analysis and documentation, including facilitation of workshops with technical and non‑technical stakeholders.
Experience translating business needs into clear, structured requirements suitable for system design and delivery.
Ability to work collaboratively across multidisciplinary teams, balancing operational realities with program and technology constraints.
High‑level written and verbal communication skills, including preparation of formal analysis artefacts and briefing material.

Desirable

Experience working with laboratory, scientific, or highly technical operational environments.
Familiarity with LIMS, diagnostic workflows, or regulated quality frameworks (e.g. ISO/IEC 17025).
Experience working within Australian Government programs or large‑scale public sector ICT projects.
Understanding of governance, assurance, and benefits realisation in program environments.

Job Title: Senior Data Engineer – Snowflake / dbt / Airflow
Ad Text:
Senior Data Engineer – Snowflake / dbt / Airflow


Location: Sydney or Melbourne Engagement Type: Contract Seniority: Senior Company: Software@Scale

About Software@Scale

Software@Scale partners with enterprise clients to provide senior, hands-on engineering, cloud, data and platform specialists who can deliver complex technology outcomes at scale.

We work across financial services, enterprise technology and regulated industries, bringing deep technical capability, strong delivery discipline and pragmatic engineering leadership into high-impact client programs.

About the Role

We are looking for a Senior Data Engineer with strong hands-on experience across Snowflake, dbt and Airflow to support a major enterprise data platform initiative.

This role is suited to someone who can design, build and optimise modern data pipelines, transformation frameworks and governed data products in a complex enterprise environment. You will work across ingestion, orchestration, ELT, data modelling, data quality, performance optimisation and operational reliability.

The ideal candidate will be highly proficient in Snowflake engineering, dbt modelling, Airflow orchestration, SQL and Python, with experience delivering production-grade data solutions in banking, financial services or another regulated enterprise environment.

AWS experience is beneficial, but the core focus of this role is modern data engineering across Snowflake, dbt and Airflow.

Key Responsibilities
Design, build and optimise scalable data pipelines across Snowflake-based data platforms.
Develop robust ELT transformation frameworks using dbt, including staging, intermediate and mart layers.
Build reusable dbt models, macros, tests, documentation and lineage to improve maintainability and transparency.
Use Airflow to orchestrate data workflows, manage dependencies, schedule pipelines and support production reliability.
Work with large, complex and semi-structured datasets across enterprise source systems.
Design and implement Snowflake data models, schemas, warehouses, tasks, streams and performance optimisation patterns.
Support data warehouse modernisation, legacy migration and cloud data platform uplift initiatives.
Implement data quality controls, validation checks, reconciliation logic, freshness checks and exception handling.
Build and maintain CI/CD practices for data pipelines and transformation logic.
Optimise Snowflake workloads for performance, reliability and cost efficiency.
Contribute to data governance, lineage, observability, monitoring and operational support practices.
Translate business and technical requirements into well-designed data engineering solutions.
Work closely with architecture, engineering, analytics, governance and business stakeholders.
Produce clear technical documentation, including pipeline designs, data mappings, runbooks and operational notes.
Core Skills & Experience
Strong commercial experience as a Senior Data Engineer, Data Platform Engineer or Analytics Engineer.
Deep hands-on experience with Snowflake in a production environment.
Strong experience building and maintaining dbt transformation layers.
Practical experience with Airflow for workflow orchestration and pipeline scheduling.
Advanced SQL skills, including complex transformations, performance tuning and analytical query design.
Strong Python experience for data engineering, automation and pipeline development.
Experience designing ETL / ELT pipelines across batch and near-real-time data workloads.
Strong understanding of data warehousing, dimensional modelling and Medallion-style data architecture.
Experience with dbt testing, documentation, lineage, macros and reusable transformation frameworks.
Experience implementing data quality, reconciliation, freshness and validation controls.
Experience with CI/CD, Git-based delivery and automated deployment practices.
Strong focus on production reliability, observability, monitoring and supportability.
Ability to work in a complex enterprise environment with multiple stakeholders and systems.
Strong communication skills and the ability to explain technical concepts clearly.
Highly Regarded
Experience in banking, financial services, insurance or another regulated enterprise environment.
Snowflake SnowPro certification or equivalent practical Snowflake expertise.
Experience with data contracts, data mesh-aligned architecture or domain-owned data products.
Experience with performance and cost optimisation across Snowflake workloads.
Experience with semi-structured data, schema evolution and automated ingestion frameworks.
